input.content:
# Phase 3 — Metrics Engine Design Spec

**Date:** 2026-04-18
**Phase:** 3 — Metrics Engine
**Scope:** Calculating, storing, and serving developer and team metrics from ingested GitHub + Jira data

---

## Overview

Phase 3 transforms raw ingested data (commits, PRs, reviews, Jira issues, changelogs, sprints) into meaningful developer and team metrics. Metrics are pre-materialized into a snapshot table, refreshed via a hybrid trigger + nightly schedule, and served via a typed query API.

We start with Tier 1 (pure SQL aggregations). Tiers 2–4 are defined in the tier classification below and will each get their own spec.

---

## Metric Tier Classification

### Tier 1 — Pure SQL Aggregations
> Data is in the DB. Aggregate with GROUP BY + time window. Implemented first.

**GitHub:**
- Commits per period, lines added / deleted / net
- PRs opened, merged, abandoned, draft rate
- PR size (lines avg, files avg)
- Reviews given, review state distribution, inline comments

**Jira:**
- Tickets created / resolved / assigned
- Bug rate, ticket type distribution
- Story points committed vs. completed per sprint
- Sprint completion rate, velocity (rolling 3-sprint avg)
- Comment volume avg, worklog time logged

### Tier 2 — Multi-Step / Time-Window Calculations
> Require changelog traversal or interval math per row.

**GitHub:** PR cycle time, time to first review, time to merge after approval, review response time, review thread resolution rate, code churn rate

**Jira:** Lead time, cycle time, time-in-status, blocked time, estimation accuracy, handoff count, re-pointing frequency, spillover count, scope change rate

### Tier 3 — Cross-Source Metrics
> Require linking Jira issue keys found in commit messages / PR bodies to Jira issues.

End-to-end lead time, ticket-to-code time (MTTD), code-to-review time, review-to-merge time, rework attribution

### Tier 4 — Derived / Composite Scores
> Weighted formulas on top of Tier 1–3 values. Product differentiators.

Collaboration Score, Velocity Consistency Score, Review Quality Score, Burnout Risk Index, PR Health Score, Estimation Accuracy Score, Impact Score, Team Health Index

---

## Key Design Decisions

| Decision | Choice | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| Storage | Pre-materialized snapshots | Dashboards query dozens of metrics at once — raw table queries won't scale |
| Granularity | Daily + Weekly + Monthly | Full flexibility, manageable at B2B scale with proper indexing |
| Teams | Admin-defined (`teams` + `team_members` tables) | Explicit, clean; GitHub team import can come later |
| Snapshot schema | Single EAV table with subject discriminator | Unified query interface for dev and team metrics; no migrations for new metric keys |
| Computation timing | Hybrid: trigger-based (daily, current period) + nightly cron (weekly/monthly rollups) | Near-real-time freshness where it matters; stable historical data |

---

## Database Schema

### `teams`
```
_id           UUID PRIMARY KEY
company_id    UUID NOT NULL → companies._id
name          VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL
created_by    UUID NOT NULL → users._id
created_at    TIMESTAMP
updated_at    TIMESTAMP
deleted_at    TIMESTAMP (soft delete)

UNIQUE (company_id, name) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL
```

### `team_members`
```
_id               UUID PRIMARY KEY
team_id           UUID NOT NULL → teams._id
platform_user_id  UUID NOT NULL → users._id
created_at        TIMESTAMP

UNIQUE (team_id, platform_user_id)
INDEX  (platform_user_id)
```

### `metric_snapshots`
```
_id            UUID PRIMARY KEY
company_id     UUID NOT NULL → companies._id
subject_type   VARCHAR(16) NOT NULL  ('developer' | 'team')
subject_id     UUID NOT NULL         (platform_user_id or team_id)
metric_key     VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL  e.g. 'gh_commits_count'
granularity    VARCHAR(8) NOT NULL   ('daily' | 'weekly' | 'monthly')
period_start   DATE NOT NULL         (start of day / ISO week Monday / month)
value_numeric  NUMERIC               scalar values
value_json     JSONB                 distributions or breakdowns
computed_at    TIMESTAMP NOT NULL

UNIQUE (company_id, subject_type, subject_id, metric_key, granularity, period_start)
INDEX  (company_id, metric_key, granularity, period_start)  -- cross-subject comparisons
```

---

## Module Structure

Flat file structure inside each module (no subdirectories).

### `teams` module
```
src/modules/teams/
  teams.controller.ts       REST endpoints — create/update/delete team, add/remove members
  teams.service.ts          business logic
  teams.model.ts            DB queries for teams table
  team-members.model.ts     DB queries for team_members table
  team.interface.ts         types
  create-team.dto.ts
  add-member.dto.ts
```

### `metrics` module
```
src/modules/metrics/
  metrics.controller.ts           query API
  metrics-snapshot.model.ts       reads/writes for metric_snapshots
  metrics-scheduler.service.ts    nightly cron (rollups + gap fill)
  metrics-queue.processor.ts      BullMQ worker (trigger-based recompute)
  github-metrics.service.ts       GitHub Tier 1 computation
  jira-metrics.service.ts         Jira Tier 1 computation
  team-metrics.service.ts         aggregates dev snapshots → team snapshots
  metric-snapshot.interface.ts    types
  metrics-query.dto.ts            query validation
```

---

## Tier 1 Metric Keys

Naming convention: `{source}_{noun}_{stat}`.

### GitHub — developer level
| metric_key | Description |
|---|---|
| `gh_commits_count` | commits authored in period |
| `gh_lines_added` | total additions |
| `gh_lines_deleted` | total deletions |
| `gh_lines_net` | additions − deletions |
| `gh_prs_opened` | PRs created |
| `gh_prs_merged` | PRs merged |
| `gh_prs_abandoned` | PRs closed without merge |
| `gh_pr_draft_rate` | draft PRs / total PRs opened |
| `gh_pr_size_lines_avg` | avg (additions + deletions) per PR |
| `gh_pr_size_files_avg` | avg changed_files per PR |
| `gh_reviews_given` | reviews submitted |
| `gh_review_approved_count` | APPROVED reviews given |
| `gh_review_changes_requested_count` | CHANGES_REQUESTED reviews given |
| `gh_review_commented_count` | COMMENTED reviews given |
| `gh_inline_comments_count` | review comments (line-level) left |

### Jira — developer level
| metric_key | Description |
|---|---|
| `jira_tickets_created` | issues created |
| `jira_tickets_resolved` | issues resolved (resolutiondate in period) |
| `jira_tickets_assigned` | issues assigned to developer |
| `jira_bug_rate` | bugs / total tickets assigned |
| `jira_story_points_committed` | points at sprint start |
| `jira_story_points_completed` | points completed at sprint end |
| `jira_sprint_completion_rate` | completed / committed (per sprint, then averaged) |
| `jira_velocity` | rolling 3-sprint avg of completed story points |
| `jira_comment_volume_avg` | avg comments per assigned ticket |
| `jira_time_logged_seconds` | total worklog seconds in period |

### Team-level
Team metrics are aggregations of the developer metrics above:
- `gh_commits_count`, `gh_lines_*`, `gh_prs_*`, `gh_reviews_given`, `gh_inline_comments_count` → **sum** across members
- `gh_pr_draft_rate`, `gh_pr_size_*_avg` → **avg** across members
- `jira_bug_rate`, `jira_sprint_completion_rate`, `jira_velocity`, `jira_comment_volume_avg` → **avg** across members
- `jira_tickets_*`, `jira_story_points_*`, `jira_time_logged_seconds` → **sum** across members

---

## Computation Pipeline

### Trigger-based (current daily snapshot)

```
GitHub/Jira webhook arrives
  → ingestion handler saves raw data (existing)
  → enqueues metrics job:
      { company_id, subject_id, subject_type: 'developer',
        granularity: 'daily', period_start: today }
  → MetricsQueueProcessor picks it up
  → calls GithubMetricsService.compute() + JiraMetricsService.compute()
  → upserts results into metric_snapshots
  → enqueues team recompute for any teams the developer belongs to
```

### Nightly cron (rollups + gap fill) — 00:00 UTC

```
For each active company:
  For each developer with at least one identity mapping:
    → recompute weekly snapshot for current ISO week
    → recompute monthly snapshot for current month
    → fill any missing daily snapshots from last 7 days
  For each team:
    → aggregate updated dev snapshots → team snapshot
```

### Computation service contract

```typescript
compute({
  companyId: string,
  platformUserId: string,
  installationIds: string[],   // all installations for the company
  periodStart: Date,
  periodEnd: Date,
  granularity: 'daily' | 'weekly' | 'monthly',
}): Promise<Map<string, number | object>>
```

All writes use `ON CONFLICT (unique index) DO UPDATE SET value_numeric, value_json, computed_at` — idempotent, safe to rerun.

---

## API

### Query developer metrics
```
GET /metrics/developer/:platformUserId
  ?metric_keys=gh_commits_count,gh_prs_merged,jira_velocity
  &granularity=weekly
  &from=2026-01-01
  &to=2026-04-18
```

### Query team metrics
```
GET /metrics/team/:teamId
  ?metric_keys=gh_prs_merged,jira_sprint_completion_rate
  &granularity=monthly
  &from=2026-01-01
  &to=2026-04-18
```

### Response shape
```json
{
  "subject_id": "uuid",
  "subject_type": "developer",
  "granularity": "weekly",
  "metrics": {
    "gh_commits_count": [
      { "period_start": "2026-04-14", "value": 12 },
      { "period_start": "2026-04-07", "value": 9 }
    ],
    "jira_velocity": [
      { "period_start": "2026-04-14", "value": 21 }
    ]
  }
}
```

---

## Teams API (admin-only)

```
POST   /teams                     create team
PATCH  /teams/:teamId             rename team
DELETE /teams/:teamId             soft delete
POST   /teams/:teamId/members     add member (body: { platform_user_id })
DELETE /teams/:teamId/members/:userId   remove member
GET    /teams                     list company teams
GET    /teams/:teamId/members     list members
```

---

## Notes

- Developers with no GitHub identity mapping produce null GitHub metrics (not zero).
- Developers with no Jira identity mapping produce null Jira metrics.
- Team snapshots are only computed if the team has at least one member with a mapping.
- Design is intentionally flexible — metric keys, aggregation rules, and API shape may be adjusted as Tier 2–4 are designed.

# Phase 3 — Metrics Engine Design Spec

**Date:** 2026-04-18
**Phase:** 3 — Metrics Engine
**Scope:** Calculating, storing, and serving developer and team metrics from ingested GitHub + Jira data

---

## Overview

Phase 3 transforms raw ingested data (commits, PRs, reviews, Jira issues, changelogs, sprints) into meaningful developer and team metrics. Metrics are pre-materialized into a snapshot table, refreshed via a hybrid trigger + nightly schedule, and served via a typed query API.

We start with Tier 1 (pure SQL aggregations). Tiers 2–4 are defined in the tier classification below and will each get their own spec.

---

## Metric Tier Classification

### Tier 1 — Pure SQL Aggregations
> Data is in the DB. Aggregate with GROUP BY + time window. Implemented first.

**GitHub:**
- Commits per period, lines added / deleted / net
- PRs opened, merged, abandoned, draft rate
- PR size (lines avg, files avg)
- Reviews given, review state distribution, inline comments

**Jira:**
- Tickets created / resolved / assigned
- Bug rate, ticket type distribution
- Story points committed vs. completed per sprint
- Sprint completion rate, velocity (rolling 3-sprint avg)
- Comment volume avg, worklog time logged

### Tier 2 — Multi-Step / Time-Window Calculations
> Require changelog traversal or interval math per row.

**GitHub:** PR cycle time, time to first review, time to merge after approval, review response time, review thread resolution rate, code churn rate

**Jira:** Lead time, cycle time, time-in-status, blocked time, estimation accuracy, handoff count, re-pointing frequency, spillover count, scope change rate

### Tier 3 — Cross-Source Metrics
> Require linking Jira issue keys found in commit messages / PR bodies to Jira issues.

End-to-end lead time, ticket-to-code time (MTTD), code-to-review time, review-to-merge time, rework attribution

### Tier 4 — Derived / Composite Scores
> Weighted formulas on top of Tier 1–3 values. Product differentiators.

Collaboration Score, Velocity Consistency Score, Review Quality Score, Burnout Risk Index, PR Health Score, Estimation Accuracy Score, Impact Score, Team Health Index

---

## Key Design Decisions

| Decision | Choice | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| Storage | Pre-materialized snapshots | Dashboards query dozens of metrics at once — raw table queries won't scale |
| Granularity | Daily + Weekly + Monthly | Full flexibility, manageable at B2B scale with proper indexing |
| Teams | Admin-defined (`teams` + `team_members` tables) | Explicit, clean; GitHub team import can come later |
| Snapshot schema | Single EAV table with subject discriminator | Unified query interface for dev and team metrics; no migrations for new metric keys |
| Computation timing | Hybrid: trigger-based (daily, current period) + nightly cron (weekly/monthly rollups) | Near-real-time freshness where it matters; stable historical data |

---

## Database Schema

### `teams`
```
_id           UUID PRIMARY KEY
company_id    UUID NOT NULL → companies._id
name          VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL
created_by    UUID NOT NULL → users._id
created_at    TIMESTAMP
updated_at    TIMESTAMP
deleted_at    TIMESTAMP (soft delete)

UNIQUE (company_id, name) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL
```

### `team_members`
```
_id               UUID PRIMARY KEY
team_id           UUID NOT NULL → teams._id
platform_user_id  UUID NOT NULL → users._id
created_at        TIMESTAMP

UNIQUE (team_id, platform_user_id)
INDEX  (platform_user_id)
```

### `metric_snapshots`
```
_id            UUID PRIMARY KEY
company_id     UUID NOT NULL → companies._id
subject_type   VARCHAR(16) NOT NULL  ('developer' | 'team')
subject_id     UUID NOT NULL         (platform_user_id or team_id)
metric_key     VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL  e.g. 'gh_commits_count'
granularity    VARCHAR(8) NOT NULL   ('daily' | 'weekly' | 'monthly')
period_start   DATE NOT NULL         (start of day / ISO week Monday / month)
value_numeric  NUMERIC               scalar values
value_json     JSONB                 distributions or breakdowns
computed_at    TIMESTAMP NOT NULL

UNIQUE (company_id, subject_type, subject_id, metric_key, granularity, period_start)
INDEX  (company_id, metric_key, granularity, period_start)  -- cross-subject comparisons
```

---

## Module Structure

Flat file structure inside each module (no subdirectories).

### `teams` module
```
src/modules/teams/
  teams.controller.ts       REST endpoints — create/update/delete team, add/remove members
  teams.service.ts          business logic
  teams.model.ts            DB queries for teams table
  team-members.model.ts     DB queries for team_members table
  team.interface.ts         types
  create-team.dto.ts
  add-member.dto.ts
```

### `metrics` module
```
src/modules/metrics/
  metrics.controller.ts           query API
  metrics-snapshot.model.ts       reads/writes for metric_snapshots
  metrics-scheduler.service.ts    nightly cron (rollups + gap fill)
  metrics-queue.processor.ts      BullMQ worker (trigger-based recompute)
  github-metrics.service.ts       GitHub Tier 1 computation
  jira-metrics.service.ts         Jira Tier 1 computation
  team-metrics.service.ts         aggregates dev snapshots → team snapshots
  metric-snapshot.interface.ts    types
  metrics-query.dto.ts            query validation
```

---

## Tier 1 Metric Keys

Naming convention: `{source}_{noun}_{stat}`.

### GitHub — developer level
| metric_key | Description |
|---|---|
| `gh_commits_count` | commits authored in period |
| `gh_lines_added` | total additions |
| `gh_lines_deleted` | total deletions |
| `gh_lines_net` | additions − deletions |
| `gh_prs_opened` | PRs created |
| `gh_prs_merged` | PRs merged |
| `gh_prs_abandoned` | PRs closed without merge |
| `gh_pr_draft_rate` | draft PRs / total PRs opened |
| `gh_pr_size_lines_avg` | avg (additions + deletions) per PR |
| `gh_pr_size_files_avg` | avg changed_files per PR |
| `gh_reviews_given` | reviews submitted |
| `gh_review_approved_count` | APPROVED reviews given |
| `gh_review_changes_requested_count` | CHANGES_REQUESTED reviews given |
| `gh_review_commented_count` | COMMENTED reviews given |
| `gh_inline_comments_count` | review comments (line-level) left |

### Jira — developer level
| metric_key | Description |
|---|---|
| `jira_tickets_created` | issues created |
| `jira_tickets_resolved` | issues resolved (resolutiondate in period) |
| `jira_tickets_assigned` | issues assigned to developer |
| `jira_bug_rate` | bugs / total tickets assigned |
| `jira_story_points_committed` | points at sprint start |
| `jira_story_points_completed` | points completed at sprint end |
| `jira_sprint_completion_rate` | completed / committed (per sprint, then averaged) |
| `jira_velocity` | rolling 3-sprint avg of completed story points |
| `jira_comment_volume_avg` | avg comments per assigned ticket |
| `jira_time_logged_seconds` | total worklog seconds in period |

### Team-level
Team metrics are aggregations of the developer metrics above:
- `gh_commits_count`, `gh_lines_*`, `gh_prs_*`, `gh_reviews_given`, `gh_inline_comments_count` → **sum** across members
- `gh_pr_draft_rate`, `gh_pr_size_*_avg` → **avg** across members
- `jira_bug_rate`, `jira_sprint_completion_rate`, `jira_velocity`, `jira_comment_volume_avg` → **avg** across members
- `jira_tickets_*`, `jira_story_points_*`, `jira_time_logged_seconds` → **sum** across members

---

## Computation Pipeline

### Trigger-based (current daily snapshot)

```
GitHub/Jira webhook arrives
  → ingestion handler saves raw data (existing)
  → enqueues metrics job:
      { company_id, subject_id, subject_type: 'developer',
        granularity: 'daily', period_start: today }
  → MetricsQueueProcessor picks it up
  → calls GithubMetricsService.compute() + JiraMetricsService.compute()
  → upserts results into metric_snapshots
  → enqueues team recompute for any teams the developer belongs to
```

### Nightly cron (rollups + gap fill) — 00:00 UTC

```
For each active company:
  For each developer with at least one identity mapping:
    → recompute weekly snapshot for current ISO week
    → recompute monthly snapshot for current month
    → fill any missing daily snapshots from last 7 days
  For each team:
    → aggregate updated dev snapshots → team snapshot
```

### Computation service contract

```typescript
compute({
  companyId: string,
  platformUserId: string,
  installationIds: string[],   // all installations for the company
  periodStart: Date,
  periodEnd: Date,
  granularity: 'daily' | 'weekly' | 'monthly',
}): Promise<Map<string, number | object>>
```

All writes use `ON CONFLICT (unique index) DO UPDATE SET value_numeric, value_json, computed_at` — idempotent, safe to rerun.

---

## API

### Query developer metrics
```
GET /metrics/developer/:platformUserId
  ?metric_keys=gh_commits_count,gh_prs_merged,jira_velocity
  &granularity=weekly
  &from=2026-01-01
  &to=2026-04-18
```

### Query team metrics
```
GET /metrics/team/:teamId
  ?metric_keys=gh_prs_merged,jira_sprint_completion_rate
  &granularity=monthly
  &from=2026-01-01
  &to=2026-04-18
```

### Response shape
```json
{
  "subject_id": "uuid",
  "subject_type": "developer",
  "granularity": "weekly",
  "metrics": {
    "gh_commits_count": [
      { "period_start": "2026-04-14", "value": 12 },
      { "period_start": "2026-04-07", "value": 9 }
    ],
    "jira_velocity": [
      { "period_start": "2026-04-14", "value": 21 }
    ]
  }
}
```

---

## Teams API (admin-only)

```
POST   /teams                     create team
PATCH  /teams/:teamId             rename team
DELETE /teams/:teamId             soft delete
POST   /teams/:teamId/members     add member (body: { platform_user_id })
DELETE /teams/:teamId/members/:userId   remove member
GET    /teams                     list company teams
GET    /teams/:teamId/members     list members
```

---

## Notes

- Developers with no GitHub identity mapping produce null GitHub metrics (not zero).
- Developers with no Jira identity mapping produce null Jira metrics.
- Team snapshots are only computed if the team has at least one member with a mapping.
- Design is intentionally flexible — metric keys, aggregation rules, and API shape may be adjusted as Tier 2–4 are designed.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Phase 3 — Metrics Engine Design Spec

**Date:** 2026-04-18
**Phase:** 3 — Metrics Engine
**Scope:** Calculating, storing, and serving developer and team metrics from ingested GitHub + Jira data

---

## Overview

Phase 3 transforms raw ingested data (commits, PRs, reviews, Jira issues, changelogs, sprints) into meaningful developer and team metrics. Metrics are computed on-demand or via scheduled jobs, cached in PostgreSQL, and served via a metrics API.

All raw data is already in the DB from Phase 2. This phase adds:
1. A `metrics` module with computation services per domain
2. A metrics cache/materialized table layer
3. API endpoints to query metrics per developer, team, or company

---

## Metric Tier Classification

Metrics are grouped into four implementation tiers based on complexity.

### Tier 1 — Pure SQL Aggregations
> Data is in the DB. Just aggregate with GROUP BY + time window.

**GitHub:**
- Commits per day/week/month (per developer)
- Lines added / deleted / net per period
- PRs opened, merged, abandoned per period
- PR draft rate
- PR size (lines, files, commits)
- Reviews given per period
- Review state distribution (APPROVED / CHANGES_REQUESTED / COMMENTED)
- Inline comments left per review

**Jira:**
- Tickets created / resolved per period
- Tickets assigned per developer
- Ticket type distribution (Story / Bug / Task / Epic)
- Priority distribution
- Bug rate (bugs / total tickets)
- Story points committed vs. completed per sprint
- Sprint completion rate
- Velocity (rolling N-sprint average of completed points)
- Comment volume per ticket
- Sub-task completion rate
- Worklog time logged per developer

---

### Tier 2 — Multi-Step / Time-Window Calculations
> Require changelog traversal, per-row logic, or interval math.

**GitHub:**
- PR cycle time (`merged_at - created_at`)
- PR time to first review (first review `submitted_at - PR.created_at`)
- PR time to merge after approval (`merged_at - last_approval.submitted_at`)
- Review response time (`review.submitted_at - review_requested_at`)
- Review thread resolution rate
- Code churn rate (lines rewritten within N days of writing)

**Jira:**
- Lead time per ticket (`resolutiondate - created`)
- Cycle time per ticket (first "In Progress" → `resolutiondate`, via changelog)
- Time in each status (consecutive changelog entries)
- Blocked time (time in "Blocked" / "Impediment" status)
- Estimation accuracy (`workratio` or `timeSpent / originalEstimate`)
- Handoff count per ticket (distinct assignee changes)
- Re-pointing frequency (story points changelog count)
- Spillover count (incomplete issues at sprint end)
- Scope change rate (issues added/removed mid-sprint)

---

### Tier 3 — Cross-Source Metrics
> Require linking Jira issue keys to GitHub commits / PRs.
> Depend on Jira issue key pattern in commit messages and PR bodies.

| Metric | Formula |
|--------|---------|
| End-to-end lead time | `PR.merged_at - Jira.issue.created` |
| Ticket-to-code time (MTTD) | `first_commit.committed_at - Jira.issue.created` |
| Code-to-review time | `PR.created_at - first_commit_in_PR.committed_at` |
| Review-to-merge time | `PR.merged_at - first_review.submitted_at` |
| Rework attribution | PRs opened for re-opened Jira issues |

---

### Tier 4 — Derived / Composite Scores
> Weighted formulas built on top of Tier 1–3 values. These are the product differentiators.

| Score | Inputs |
|-------|--------|
| Collaboration Score (0–100) | Reviews given ratio, comment depth, thread resolution, cross-team review rate, Jira comment participation, review response time |
| Velocity Consistency Score | `1 - (stddev / mean)` of weekly output (story points + PRs + commits, normalized) |
| Review Quality Score | Comment/approval ratio, thread follow-up, actionable comment rate, review depth |
| Burnout Risk Index (LOW/MED/HIGH/CRITICAL) | After-hours ratio, weekend activity, output variance, streak length, declining trend, PR cycle time increase, Jira blocked time |
| PR Health Score | Size, linked ticket, description, review coverage, CI pass, no force push |
| Estimation Accuracy Score | `1 - abs(actual - estimate) / estimate`, per dev per sprint |
| Impact Score | Composite of delivery output, code contribution, review contribution, collaboration, reliability, security hygiene, mentorship signal |
| Team Health Index | Aggregate of cycle time trend, burnout risk avg, sprint completion trend, WIP violations, review participation, blocked time, scope change, velocity trend |

---

## What We Build First

Starting with **Tier 1** — pure SQL aggregations. These are the foundation everything else depends on and the fastest path to a working metrics API.

Tier 1 design details will be fleshed out in subsequent sections as each metric group is discussed and approved.

---

## Open Questions (to be resolved per tier)

- How are metrics stored — computed on-demand vs. pre-materialized in a `metrics_snapshots` table?
- What time granularity do we expose — daily / weekly / monthly?
- How do we handle developers with no Jira or no GitHub data (partial identity mapping)?
- What is the primary query axis — `(company_id, platform_user_id, date_range)` or per-installation?

- `jira_velocity` and `jira_sprint_completion_rate` are only meaningful at monthly granularity. Daily/weekly snapshots for these keys are not computed.
- Design is intentionally flexible — metric keys, aggregation rules, and API shape may be adjusted as Tier 2–4 are designed.

- Soft-deleting a team does not delete `team_members` rows — they become inert when the team is soft-deleted. No cascade needed.
- `jira_velocity` and `jira_sprint_completion_rate` are only meaningful at monthly granularity. Daily/weekly snapshots for these keys are not computed.

lines: - Developers with no GitHub identity mapping produce null GitHub metrics (not zero).
- Developers with no Jira identity mapping produce null Jira metrics.
- Team snapshots are only computed if the team has at least one member with a mapping.
+- Soft-deleting a team does not delete `team_members` rows — they become inert when the team is soft-deleted. No cascade needed.
+- `jira_velocity` and `jira_sprint_completion_rate` are only meaningful at monthly granularity. Daily/weekly snapshots for these keys are not computed.
- Design is intentionally flexible — metric keys, aggregation rules, and API shape may be adjusted as Tier 2–4 are designed.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/4thwithme/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/5.0.6/skills/writing-plans

# Writing Plans

## Overview

Write comprehensive implementation plans assuming the engineer has zero context for our codebase and questionable taste. Document everything they need to know: which files to touch for each task, code, testing, docs they might need to check, how to test it. Give them the whole plan as bite-sized tasks. DRY. YAGNI. TDD. Frequent commits.

Assume they are a skilled developer, but know almost nothing about our toolset or problem domain. Assume they don't know good test design very well.

**Announce at start:** "I'm using the writing-plans skill to create the implementation plan."

**Context:** This should be run in a dedicated worktree (created by brainstorming skill).

**Save plans to:** `docs/superpowers/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<feature-name>.md`
- (User preferences for plan location override this default)

## Scope Check

If the spec covers multiple independent subsystems, it should have been broken into sub-project specs during brainstorming. If it wasn't, suggest breaking this into separate plans — one per subsystem. Each plan should produce working, testable software on its own.

## File Structure

Before defining tasks, map out which files will be created or modified and what each one is responsible for. This is where decomposition decisions get locked in.

- Design units with clear boundaries and well-defined interfaces. Each file should have one clear responsibility.
- You reason best about code you can hold in context at once, and your edits are more reliable when files are focused. Prefer smaller, focused files over large ones that do too much.
- Files that change together should live together. Split by responsibility, not by technical layer.
- In existing codebases, follow established patterns. If the codebase uses large files, don't unilaterally restructure - but if a file you're modifying has grown unwieldy, including a split in the plan is reasonable.

This structure informs the task decomposition. Each task should produce self-contained changes that make sense independently.

## Bite-Sized Task Granularity

**Each step is one action (2-5 minutes):**
- "Write the failing test" - step
- "Run it to make sure it fails" - step
- "Implement the minimal code to make the test pass" - step
- "Run the tests and make sure they pass" - step
- "Commit" - step

## Plan Document Header

**Every plan MUST start with this header:**

```markdown
# [Feature Name] Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** [One sentence describing what this builds]

**Architecture:** [2-3 sentences about approach]

**Tech Stack:** [Key technologies/libraries]

---
```

## Task Structure

````markdown
### Task N: [Component Name]

**Files:**
- Create: `exact/path/to/file.py`
- Modify: `exact/path/to/existing.py:123-145`
- Test: `tests/exact/path/to/test.py`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing test**

```python
def test_specific_behavior():
    result = function(input)
    assert result == expected
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run test to verify it fails**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_name -v`
Expected: FAIL with "function not defined"

- [ ] **Step 3: Write minimal implementation**

```python
def function(input):
    return expected
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Run test to verify it passes**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_name -v`
Expected: PASS

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add tests/path/test.py src/path/file.py
git commit -m "feat: add specific feature"
```
````

## No Placeholders

Every step must contain the actual content an engineer needs. These are **plan failures** — never write them:
- "TBD", "TODO", "implement later", "fill in details"
- "Add appropriate error handling" / "add validation" / "handle edge cases"
- "Write tests for the above" (without actual test code)
- "Similar to Task N" (repeat the code — the engineer may be reading tasks out of order)
- Steps that describe what to do without showing how (code blocks required for code steps)
- References to types, functions, or methods not defined in any task

## Remember
- Exact file paths always
- Complete code in every step — if a step changes code, show the code
- Exact commands with expected output
- DRY, YAGNI, TDD, frequent commits

## Self-Review

After writing the complete plan, look at the spec with fresh eyes and check the plan against it. This is a checklist you run yourself — not a subagent dispatch.

**1. Spec coverage:** Skim each section/requirement in the spec. Can you point to a task that implements it? List any gaps.

**2. Placeholder scan:** Search your plan for red flags — any of the patterns from the "No Placeholders" section above. Fix them.

**3. Type consistency:** Do the types, method signatures, and property names you used in later tasks match what you defined in earlier tasks? A function called `clearLayers()` in Task 3 but `clearFullLayers()` in Task 7 is a bug.

If you find issues, fix them inline. No need to re-review — just fix and move on. If you find a spec requirement with no task, add the task.

## Execution Handoff

After saving the plan, offer execution choice:

**"Plan complete and saved to `docs/superpowers/plans/<filename>.md`. Two execution options:**

**1. Subagent-Driven (recommended)** - I dispatch a fresh subagent per task, review between tasks, fast iteration

**2. Inline Execution** - Execute tasks in this session using executing-plans, batch execution with checkpoints

**Which approach?"**

**If Subagent-Driven chosen:**
- **REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development
- Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review

**If Inline Execution chosen:**
- **REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** Use superpowers:executing-plans
- Batch execution with checkpoints for review

file.content:
# Phase 3 — Metrics Engine Design Spec

**Date:** 2026-04-18
**Phase:** 3 — Metrics Engine
**Scope:** Calculating, storing, and serving developer and team metrics from ingested GitHub + Jira data

---

## Overview

Phase 3 transforms raw ingested data (commits, PRs, reviews, Jira issues, changelogs, sprints) into meaningful developer and team metrics. Metrics are pre-materialized into a snapshot table, refreshed via a hybrid trigger + nightly schedule, and served via a typed query API.

We start with Tier 1 (pure SQL aggregations). Tiers 2–4 are defined in the tier classification below and will each get their own spec.

---

## Metric Tier Classification

### Tier 1 — Pure SQL Aggregations
> Data is in the DB. Aggregate with GROUP BY + time window. Implemented first.

**GitHub:**
- Commits per period, lines added / deleted / net
- PRs opened, merged, abandoned, draft rate
- PR size (lines avg, files avg)
- Reviews given, review state distribution, inline comments

**Jira:**
- Tickets created / resolved / assigned
- Bug rate, ticket type distribution
- Story points committed vs. completed per sprint
- Sprint completion rate, velocity (rolling 3-sprint avg)
- Comment volume avg, worklog time logged

### Tier 2 — Multi-Step / Time-Window Calculations
> Require changelog traversal or interval math per row.

**GitHub:** PR cycle time, time to first review, time to merge after approval, review response time, review thread resolution rate, code churn rate

**Jira:** Lead time, cycle time, time-in-status, blocked time, estimation accuracy, handoff count, re-pointing frequency, spillover count, scope change rate

### Tier 3 — Cross-Source Metrics
> Require linking Jira issue keys found in commit messages / PR bodies to Jira issues.

End-to-end lead time, ticket-to-code time (MTTD), code-to-review time, review-to-merge time, rework attribution

### Tier 4 — Derived / Composite Scores
> Weighted formulas on top of Tier 1–3 values. Product differentiators.

Collaboration Score, Velocity Consistency Score, Review Quality Score, Burnout Risk Index, PR Health Score, Estimation Accuracy Score, Impact Score, Team Health Index

---

## Key Design Decisions

| Decision | Choice | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| Storage | Pre-materialized snapshots | Dashboards query dozens of metrics at once — raw table queries won't scale |
| Granularity | Daily + Weekly + Monthly | Full flexibility, manageable at B2B scale with proper indexing |
| Teams | Admin-defined (`teams` + `team_members` tables) | Explicit, clean; GitHub team import can come later |
| Snapshot schema | Single EAV table with subject discriminator | Unified query interface for dev and team metrics; no migrations for new metric keys |
| Computation timing | Hybrid: trigger-based (daily, current period) + nightly cron (weekly/monthly rollups) | Near-real-time freshness where it matters; stable historical data |

---

## Database Schema

### `teams`
```
_id           UUID PRIMARY KEY
company_id    UUID NOT NULL → companies._id
name          VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL
created_by    UUID NOT NULL → users._id
created_at    TIMESTAMP
updated_at    TIMESTAMP
deleted_at    TIMESTAMP (soft delete)

UNIQUE (company_id, name) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL
```

### `team_members`
```
_id               UUID PRIMARY KEY
team_id           UUID NOT NULL → teams._id
platform_user_id  UUID NOT NULL → users._id
created_at        TIMESTAMP

UNIQUE (team_id, platform_user_id)
INDEX  (platform_user_id)
```

### `metric_snapshots`
```
_id            UUID PRIMARY KEY
company_id     UUID NOT NULL → companies._id
subject_type   VARCHAR(16) NOT NULL  ('developer' | 'team')
subject_id     UUID NOT NULL         (platform_user_id or team_id)
metric_key     VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL  e.g. 'gh_commits_count'
granularity    VARCHAR(8) NOT NULL   ('daily' | 'weekly' | 'monthly')
period_start   DATE NOT NULL         (start of day / ISO week Monday / month)
value_numeric  NUMERIC               scalar values
value_json     JSONB                 distributions or breakdowns
computed_at    TIMESTAMP NOT NULL

UNIQUE (company_id, subject_type, subject_id, metric_key, granularity, period_start)
INDEX  (company_id, metric_key, granularity, period_start)  -- cross-subject comparisons
```

---

## Module Structure

Flat file structure inside each module (no subdirectories).

### `teams` module
```
src/modules/teams/
  teams.controller.ts       REST endpoints — create/update/delete team, add/remove members
  teams.service.ts          business logic
  teams.model.ts            DB queries for teams table
  team-members.model.ts     DB queries for team_members table
  team.interface.ts         types
  create-team.dto.ts
  add-member.dto.ts
```

### `metrics` module
```
src/modules/metrics/
  metrics.controller.ts           query API
  metrics-snapshot.model.ts       reads/writes for metric_snapshots
  metrics-scheduler.service.ts    nightly cron (rollups + gap fill)
  metrics-queue.processor.ts      BullMQ worker (trigger-based recompute)
  github-metrics.service.ts       GitHub Tier 1 computation
  jira-metrics.service.ts         Jira Tier 1 computation
  team-metrics.service.ts         aggregates dev snapshots → team snapshots
  metric-snapshot.interface.ts    types
  metrics-query.dto.ts            query validation
```

---

## Tier 1 Metric Keys

Naming convention: `{source}_{noun}_{stat}`.

### GitHub — developer level
| metric_key | Description |
|---|---|
| `gh_commits_count` | commits authored in period |
| `gh_lines_added` | total additions |
| `gh_lines_deleted` | total deletions |
| `gh_lines_net` | additions − deletions |
| `gh_prs_opened` | PRs created |
| `gh_prs_merged` | PRs merged |
| `gh_prs_abandoned` | PRs closed without merge |
| `gh_pr_draft_rate` | draft PRs / total PRs opened |
| `gh_pr_size_lines_avg` | avg (additions + deletions) per PR |
| `gh_pr_size_files_avg` | avg changed_files per PR |
| `gh_reviews_given` | reviews submitted |
| `gh_review_approved_count` | APPROVED reviews given |
| `gh_review_changes_requested_count` | CHANGES_REQUESTED reviews given |
| `gh_review_commented_count` | COMMENTED reviews given |
| `gh_inline_comments_count` | review comments (line-level) left |

### Jira — developer level
| metric_key | Description |
|---|---|
| `jira_tickets_created` | issues created |
| `jira_tickets_resolved` | issues resolved (resolutiondate in period) |
| `jira_tickets_assigned` | issues assigned to developer |
| `jira_bug_rate` | bugs / total tickets assigned |
| `jira_story_points_committed` | points at sprint start |
| `jira_story_points_completed` | points completed at sprint end |
| `jira_sprint_completion_rate` | completed / committed (avg across sprints that ended in period) |
| `jira_velocity` | rolling 3-sprint avg of completed story points (stored at monthly granularity only — sprint-based, not time-window-based) |
| `jira_comment_volume_avg` | avg comments per assigned ticket |
| `jira_time_logged_seconds` | total worklog seconds in period |

### Team-level
Team metrics are aggregations of the developer metrics above:
- `gh_commits_count`, `gh_lines_*`, `gh_prs_*`, `gh_reviews_given`, `gh_inline_comments_count` → **sum** across members
- `gh_pr_draft_rate`, `gh_pr_size_*_avg` → **avg** across members
- `jira_bug_rate`, `jira_sprint_completion_rate`, `jira_velocity`, `jira_comment_volume_avg` → **avg** across members
- `jira_tickets_*`, `jira_story_points_*`, `jira_time_logged_seconds` → **sum** across members

---

## Computation Pipeline

### Trigger-based (current daily snapshot)

```
GitHub/Jira webhook arrives
  → ingestion handler saves raw data (existing)
  → enqueues metrics job:
      { company_id, subject_id, subject_type: 'developer',
        granularity: 'daily', period_start: today }
  → MetricsQueueProcessor picks it up
  → calls GithubMetricsService.compute() + JiraMetricsService.compute()
  → upserts results into metric_snapshots
  → enqueues team recompute for any teams the developer belongs to
```

### Nightly cron (rollups + gap fill) — 00:00 UTC

```
For each active company:
  For each developer with at least one identity mapping:
    → recompute weekly snapshot for current ISO week
    → recompute monthly snapshot for current month
    → fill any missing daily snapshots from last 7 days
  For each team:
    → aggregate updated dev snapshots → team snapshot
```

### Computation service contract

```typescript
compute({
  companyId: string,
  platformUserId: string,
  installationIds: string[],   // all installations for the company
  periodStart: Date,
  periodEnd: Date,
  granularity: 'daily' | 'weekly' | 'monthly',
}): Promise<Map<string, number | object>>
```

All writes use `ON CONFLICT (unique index) DO UPDATE SET value_numeric, value_json, computed_at` — idempotent, safe to rerun.

---

## API

### Query developer metrics
```
GET /metrics/developer/:platformUserId
  ?metric_keys=gh_commits_count,gh_prs_merged,jira_velocity
  &granularity=weekly
  &from=2026-01-01
  &to=2026-04-18
```

### Query team metrics
```
GET /metrics/team/:teamId
  ?metric_keys=gh_prs_merged,jira_sprint_completion_rate
  &granularity=monthly
  &from=2026-01-01
  &to=2026-04-18
```

### Response shape
```json
{
  "subject_id": "uuid",
  "subject_type": "developer",
  "granularity": "weekly",
  "metrics": {
    "gh_commits_count": [
      { "period_start": "2026-04-14", "value": 12 },
      { "period_start": "2026-04-07", "value": 9 }
    ],
    "jira_velocity": [
      { "period_start": "2026-04-14", "value": 21 }
    ]
  }
}
```

---

## Teams API (admin-only)

```
POST   /teams                     create team
PATCH  /teams/:teamId             rename team
DELETE /teams/:teamId             soft delete
POST   /teams/:teamId/members     add member (body: { platform_user_id })
DELETE /teams/:teamId/members/:userId   remove member
GET    /teams                     list company teams
GET    /teams/:teamId/members     list members
```

---

## Notes

- Developers with no GitHub identity mapping produce null GitHub metrics (not zero).
- Developers with no Jira identity mapping produce null Jira metrics.
- Team snapshots are only computed if the team has at least one member with a mapping.
- Soft-deleting a team does not delete `team_members` rows — they become inert when the team is soft-deleted. No cascade needed.
- `jira_velocity` and `jira_sprint_completion_rate` are only meaningful at monthly granularity. Daily/weekly snapshots for these keys are not computed.
- Design is intentionally flexible — metric keys, aggregation rules, and API shape may be adjusted as Tier 2–4 are designed.

Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. ## Phase 3 — Metrics Engine

Calculate raw stats and derived complex metrics from ingested data.

Full metrics catalog: [`docs/features/metrics-catalog.md`](metrics-catalog.md)
GitHub API data reference: [`docs/integrations/github-api.md`](../integrations/github-api.md)
Jira API data reference: [`docs/integrations/jira-api.md`](../integrations/jira-api.md)

### GitHub Raw Metrics (selected)
- [ ] Commit frequency, additions/deletions, after-hours rate, punch card patterns
- [ ] PR cycle time, size, draft rate, merge method distribution
- [ ] Review response time, review state distribution, thread resolution rate
- [ ] CI build success rate, build duration, flaky test rate
- [ ] Deployment frequency, lead time to production
- [ ] Security alert severity distribution, push_protection bypass tracking

### Jira Raw Metrics (selected)
- [ ] Lead time, cycle time, time-in-status per ticket (from changelog)
- [ ] Story points committed vs. completed per sprint
- [ ] Estimation accuracy (`workratio`), re-pointing frequency
- [ ] Blocked time (Flagged/Impediment), handoff count, rework rate
- [ ] Sprint scope change, spillover, velocity trend
- [ ] WIP violations vs. board limits

### Derived / Complex Metrics
- [ ] **Collaboration Score** — reviews + comments + cross-team participation
- [ ] **Mean Time to Development (MTTD)** — Jira created → first commit
- [ ] **Mean Time to Review (MTTR)** — PR opened → first review
- [ ] **Code Churn Rate** — lines rewritten within N days of writing
- [ ] **Burnout Risk Index** — after-hours + output variance + declining trend
- [ ] **Velocity Consistency Score** — stddev/mean of weekly output
- [ ] **Review Quality Score** — comment depth vs. rubber-stamp approvals
- [ ] **Impact Score** — weighted composite of all above
- [ ] **PR Health Score** — size + description + CI + review coverage
- [ ] **Team Health Index** — aggregate team-level composite
- [ ] **Estimation Accuracy Score** — per developer, per sprint, trended
- [ ] **Seniority Classification** — for global benchmark matching

### Benchmarking Data
- [ ] Anonymized aggregation pipeline (B2B data)
- [ ] Public GitHub profile scraper for global benchmarks
- [ ] Seniority classification model (based on account age, repo stars, contribution history)
- [ ] Percentile calculation per metric per seniority band

---

## Phase 3 — Metrics Engine

Calculate raw stats and derived complex metrics from ingested data.
Design spec: [`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-18-phase3-metrics-engine-design.md`](../superpowers/specs/2026-04-18-phase3-metrics-engine-design.md)
Full metrics catalog: [`docs/features/metrics-catalog.md`](metrics-catalog.md)

Metrics are implemented in four tiers of complexity. Each tier has its own implementation plan.

### Tier 1 — Pure SQL Aggregations (current)
> Infrastructure: `teams` module, `metric_snapshots` table, computation pipeline, query API.

**GitHub:**
- [ ] `gh_commits_count`, `gh_lines_added`, `gh_lines_deleted`, `gh_lines_net`
- [ ] `gh_prs_opened`, `gh_prs_merged`, `gh_prs_abandoned`, `gh_pr_draft_rate`
- [ ] `gh_pr_size_lines_avg`, `gh_pr_size_files_avg`
- [ ] `gh_reviews_given`, `gh_review_approved_count`, `gh_review_changes_requested_count`, `gh_review_commented_count`
- [ ] `gh_inline_comments_count`

**Jira:**
- [ ] `jira_tickets_created`, `jira_tickets_resolved`, `jira_tickets_assigned`
- [ ] `jira_bug_rate`, `jira_time_logged_seconds`

**Infrastructure:**
- [ ] `teams` + `team_members` tables + admin CRUD API
- [ ] `metric_snapshots` EAV table (developer + team, daily/weekly/monthly)
- [ ] Trigger-based recompute (BullMQ) + nightly cron rollup
- [ ] Metrics query API (`GET /metrics/developer/:id`, `GET /metrics/team/:id`)

---

### Tier 2 — Multi-Step / Time-Window Calculations
> Require changelog traversal or interval math per row.

**GitHub:**
- [ ] `gh_pr_cycle_time_avg` — `merged_at - opened_at`
- [ ] `gh_pr_time_to_first_review_avg` — first review `submitted_at - PR.opened_at`
- [ ] `gh_pr_time_to_merge_after_approval_avg` — `merged_at - last_approval.submitted_at`
- [ ] `gh_review_response_time_avg` — `review.submitted_at - review_requested_at`
- [ ] `gh_code_churn_rate` — lines rewritten within 14 days of writing

**Jira:**
- [ ] `jira_lead_time_avg` — `resolved_at - created_at`
- [ ] `jira_cycle_time_avg` — first "In Progress" → `resolved_at` (changelog)
- [ ] `jira_blocked_time_avg` — time in blocked/impediment status
- [ ] `jira_estimation_accuracy` — `workratio` or `timeSpent / originalEstimate`
- [ ] `jira_handoff_count_avg` — distinct assignee changes per ticket
- [ ] `jira_spillover_count` — incomplete issues at sprint end
- [ ] `jira_scope_change_rate` — issues added/removed mid-sprint

---

### Tier 3 — Cross-Source Metrics
> Require linking Jira issue keys in commit messages / PR bodies to Jira issues.

- [ ] `cross_end_to_end_lead_time` — `PR.merged_at - Jira.issue.created`
- [ ] `cross_ticket_to_code_time` — first commit `committed_at - Jira.issue.created`
- [ ] `cross_code_to_review_time` — `PR.opened_at - first_commit.committed_at`
- [ ] `cross_review_to_merge_time` — `PR.merged_at - first_review.submitted_at`
- [ ] `cross_rework_attribution` — PRs for re-opened Jira issues

---

### Tier 4 — Derived / Composite Scores
> Weighted formulas on top of Tier 1–3. Product differentiators.

- [ ] **Collaboration Score** (0–100) — reviews, comments, cross-team participation
- [ ] **Velocity Consistency Score** — `1 - stddev/mean` of weekly output
- [ ] **Review Quality Score** — comment depth, thread follow-up, actionable rate
- [ ] **Burnout Risk Index** (LOW/MED/HIGH/CRITICAL) — after-hours, variance, trends
- [ ] **PR Health Score** — size, linked ticket, description, CI, review coverage
- [ ] **Estimation Accuracy Score** — per dev per sprint, trended
- [ ] **Impact Score** — weighted composite of all above
- [ ] **Team Health Index** — aggregate team-level composite
- [ ] **Seniority Classification** — for benchmark matching

### Benchmarking Data
- [ ] Anonymized aggregation pipeline (B2B data)
- [ ] Public GitHub profile scraper for global benchmarks
- [ ] Seniority classification model
- [ ] Percentile calculation per metric per seniority band

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Feature Roadmap

## Phase 1 — Foundation (Current)

Core auth, multi-tenancy, and company management. No integrations yet.

### Completed
- [x] Auth system — GitHub + Google OAuth, JWT, Redis sessions
- [x] Multi-tenant company model — companies, members, roles (admin/member)
- [x] Invitation system — email-based, 72hr expiry, single-use
- [x] CSRF protection
- [x] Health checks, logging, rate limiting

---

## Phase 2 — Data Ingestion ✓

Connect to external data sources and store raw developer activity.

### GitHub Integration
- [x] GitHub App setup (org-level access, webhook + OAuth)
- [x] Webhook ingestion — push events, PR events, review events
- [x] Polling fallback for historical data (backfill service)
- [x] Store: commits, PRs, reviews, review comments, contributors
- [x] Contributor identity mapping (GitHub login → platform user)

### Jira Integration
- [x] Jira OAuth 2.0 setup (cloud OAuth, token refresh)
- [x] Webhook ingestion — issue created/updated events
- [x] Historical data backfill (full project sync via polling)
- [x] Store: issues, sprints, worklogs, changelogs, projects
- [x] Jira user → platform user mapping (jira_identity_mappings table)

### Data Pipeline
- [x] Queue system for ingestion jobs (BullMQ + Redis)
- [x] Idempotent event processing (upsert on conflict)
- [x] Raw event storage (postgres append-friendly tables)
- [x] Normalized data layer (typed models per entity)

---

## Phase 3 — Metrics Engine

Calculate raw stats and derived complex metrics from ingested data.

Full metrics catalog: [`docs/features/metrics-catalog.md`](metrics-catalog.md)
GitHub API data reference: [`docs/integrations/github-api.md`](../integrations/github-api.md)
Jira API data reference: [`docs/integrations/jira-api.md`](../integrations/jira-api.md)

### GitHub Raw Metrics (selected)
- [ ] Commit frequency, additions/deletions, after-hours rate, punch card patterns
- [ ] PR cycle time, size, draft rate, merge method distribution
- [ ] Review response time, review state distribution, thread resolution rate
- [ ] CI build success rate, build duration, flaky test rate
- [ ] Deployment frequency, lead time to production
- [ ] Security alert severity distribution, push_protection bypass tracking

### Jira Raw Metrics (selected)
- [ ] Lead time, cycle time, time-in-status per ticket (from changelog)
- [ ] Story points committed vs. completed per sprint
- [ ] Estimation accuracy (`workratio`), re-pointing frequency
- [ ] Blocked time (Flagged/Impediment), handoff count, rework rate
- [ ] Sprint scope change, spillover, velocity trend
- [ ] WIP violations vs. board limits

### Derived / Complex Metrics
- [ ] **Collaboration Score** — reviews + comments + cross-team participation
- [ ] **Mean Time to Development (MTTD)** — Jira created → first commit
- [ ] **Mean Time to Review (MTTR)** — PR opened → first review
- [ ] **Code Churn Rate** — lines rewritten within N days of writing
- [ ] **Burnout Risk Index** — after-hours + output variance + declining trend
- [ ] **Velocity Consistency Score** — stddev/mean of weekly output
- [ ] **Review Quality Score** — comment depth vs. rubber-stamp approvals
- [ ] **Impact Score** — weighted composite of all above
- [ ] **PR Health Score** — size + description + CI + review coverage
- [ ] **Team Health Index** — aggregate team-level composite
- [ ] **Estimation Accuracy Score** — per developer, per sprint, trended
- [ ] **Seniority Classification** — for global benchmark matching

### Benchmarking Data
- [ ] Anonymized aggregation pipeline (B2B data)
- [ ] Public GitHub profile scraper for global benchmarks
- [ ] Seniority classification model (based on account age, repo stars, contribution history)
- [ ] Percentile calculation per metric per seniority band

---

## Phase 4 — Dashboard & Visualizations

Frontend views for all comparison modes and data presentations.

### Views
- [ ] Single developer profile dashboard
- [ ] Dev vs Dev comparison
- [ ] Dev vs Team comparison
- [ ] Dev vs Global benchmark
- [ ] Team overview dashboard
- [ ] Team vs Team comparison
- [ ] Time-series views for all of the above

### Presentation Formats
- [ ] Table view (sortable, filterable, exportable)
- [ ] Line/bar charts — trends over time
- [ ] Radar chart — multi-metric profile
- [ ] Heatmap — activity calendar (GitHub-style)
- [ ] Metric cards — summary KPIs

---

## Phase 5 — AI Layer

Natural language analysis and recommendations on top of metrics.

- [ ] Per-developer AI narrative report (weekly/monthly)
- [ ] Short actionable tips per developer
- [ ] Anomaly detection with AI explanation
- [ ] Benchmark comparison narratives
- [ ] Team health summary with AI commentary
- [ ] Natural language query interface ("Who is at burnout risk?")

---

## Phase 6 — B2C Features

Individual developer self-service, independent of company accounts.

- [ ] Personal developer account (standalone, no company required)
- [ ] Connect personal GitHub account
- [ ] Personal stats dashboard
- [ ] Shareable performance card — generated image (CV insert, LinkedIn, GitHub README)
- [ ] Public developer profile page (opt-in)
- [ ] Personal AI analysis report

---

## Future / Backlog

- GitLab integration
- Linear / Shortcut / Azure DevOps integration
- Slack collaboration signals
- PR quality analysis (LLM-based code review scoring)
- 1:1 meeting prep reports for managers
- Performance review automation assist
- Mobile app
- Webhooks / API for customers to pull their own data
- SSO / SAML for enterprise

content.text: Now writing the implementation plan.

Writing the implementation plan file. It's large — needs actual TypeScript code, SQL queries, and test code for ~10 tasks covering:

- 3 DB migrations (teams, team_members, metric_snapshots)
- Teams module (models, service, controller)
- Metrics snapshot model
- GitHub metrics computation service (4 SQL aggregation queries)
- Jira metrics computation service
- Team aggregation service
- BullMQ processor
- Nightly cron scheduler
- Query API controller

Each step needs exact file paths, real code, exact commands, and expected output. No placeholders allowed. That's why it takes a minute.
